hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, two-stage MD/GCMC simulations, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to polyamine discovery, then it will efficiently identify high-performance CO2-capturing polyamines within a limited simulation budget because the surrogate models trained on molecular embeddings can accurately predict adsorption capacity and guide exploration toward optimal structures without exhaustively simulating the entire chemical space.
